{{Unreferenced|date=March 2017|sortkey=温かけゆ}} {{infobox Onsen |name = Kakeyu Onsen (鹿教湯温泉) |image = kakeyu_onsen001.jpg |comment = Hot spring town as seen from the observatory |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市}} |access = Railway: About 45 minutes by bus on the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) from the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}; alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stopIn the past, Chikuma Bus operated connecting services with the former {{ill|Japanese National Railways|ja|日本国有鉄道}}, and a shop adjacent to this bus stop sold train tickets for the national railway line, which is why the bus stop was called a “station.” Although connecting services have now been discontinued and ticket sales are no longer handled, the shop that once sold tickets remains in operation, and the bus stop’s name has remained unchanged. The signage on the bus stop pillar has been revised to “Kakeyu Onsen.” See: [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf Meeting Materials 4 – On the Bus Fare Reduction and Operation Plan (Draft)], p.21, 2013, Ueda City; [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf Ueda City Bus Route Map], as of April 1, 2021, Ueda City Transportation Policy Division.
Automobile: About 40 minutes from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} |quality = {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs}} |temperature = 42 |flow_rate = |pH = |liquid_type = |osmotic_pressure = |number_of_accommodations = |total_capacity = |annual_number_of_visitors = |statistics_year = |external_link = [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen Homepage|ja|鹿教湯温泉ホームページ}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] |notes = }} '''Kakeyu Onsen''' (鹿教湯温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市}} (formerly {{ill|Maruko Town|ja|丸子町}}, formerly {{ill|Nishiuchi Village|ja|西内村}}, formerly part of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}). == Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs}} ** {{ill|Natural spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth}} {{ill|Temperature|ja|温度}} 42℃. ** A colorless, transparent natural spring === Efficacy === It is said to be effective for strokes and high blood pressure. *Note: The stated efficacy does not guarantee results for everyone.* [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] == Hot Spring Town == [[File:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Kakeyu Onsen Ryokan Town]] Along the {{ill|Uchimura River|ja|内村川}}, about thirty ryokan and large hotels are located, along with facilities such as {{ill|Rehabilitation|ja|リハビリテーション|lt=Rehabilitation|lt_ja=リハビリ}}-centered {{ill|Kakeyu Hospital|ja|鹿教湯病院}} and the {{ill|Health Land|ja|健康ランド|lt=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|deer|ja|シカ|lt=Deer|lt_ja=鹿}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道}}. revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=March 2017|sortkey=温かけゆ}} {{infobox Onsen |name = Kakeyu Onsen (鹿教湯温泉) |image = kakeyu_onsen001.jpg |comment = Hot spring town as seen from the observatory |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市}} |access = Railway: About 45 minutes by bus on the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) from the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}}; alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stopIn the past, Chikuma Bus operated connecting services with the former {{ill|Japanese National Railways|ja|日本国有鉄道}}, and a shop adjacent to this bus stop sold train tickets for the national railway line, which is why the bus stop was called a “station.” Although connecting services have now been discontinued and ticket sales are no longer handled, the shop that once sold tickets remains in operation, and the bus stop’s name has remained unchanged. The signage on the bus stop pillar has been revised to “Kakeyu Onsen.” See: [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf Meeting Materials 4 – On the Bus Fare Reduction and Operation Plan (Draft)], p.21, 2013, Ueda City; [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf Ueda City Bus Route Map], as of April 1, 2021, Ueda City Transportation Policy Division.
Automobile: About 40 minutes from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}} |quality = {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs}} |temperature = 42 |flow_rate = |pH = |liquid_type = |osmotic_pressure = |number_of_accommodations = |total_capacity = |annual_number_of_visitors = |statistics_year = |external_link = [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen Homepage|ja|鹿教湯温泉ホームページ}]] |notes = }} '''Kakeyu Onsen''' (鹿教湯温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市}} (formerly {{ill|Maruko Town|ja|丸子町}}, formerly {{ill|Nishiuchi Village|ja|西内村}}, formerly part of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}). == Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs}} ** {{ill|Natural spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth}} {{ill|Temperature|ja|温度}} 42℃. ** A colorless, transparent natural spring === Efficacy === It is said to be effective for strokes and high blood pressure. *Note: The stated efficacy does not guarantee results for everyone.* == Hot Spring Town == [[File:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Kakeyu Onsen Ryokan Town]] Along the {{ill|Uchimura River|ja|内村川}}, about thirty ryokan and large hotels are located, along with facilities such as {{ill|Rehabilitation|ja|リハビリテーション|lt=Rehabilitation|lt_ja=リハビリ}}-centered {{ill|Kakeyu Hospital|ja|鹿教湯病院}} and the {{ill|Health Land|ja|健康ランド|lt=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|deer|ja|シカ|lt=Deer|lt_ja=鹿}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends of Kōbō Daishi]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Legends]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kakeyu Onsen}}

Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|12=draft}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|deer|ja|シカ|lt=Deer|lt_ja=鹿|12=draft}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=draft}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=draft}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=draft}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国|12=draft}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡|12=draft}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=draft}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅|12=draft}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=draft}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends of Kōbō Daishi]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Legends]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kakeyu Onsen}} {{Kofun navbar}}

{{Unreferenced|date=March 2017|sortkey=温かけゆ}} {{infobox Onsen |name = Kakeyu Onsen (鹿教湯温泉) |image = kakeyu_onsen001.jpg |comment = Hot spring town as seen from the observatory |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft}} |access = Railway: About 45 minutes by bus on the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) from the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft}}; alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stopIn the past, Chikuma Bus operated connecting services with the former {{ill|Japanese National Railways|ja|日本国有鉄道|12=draft}}, and a shop adjacent to this bus stop sold train tickets for the national railway line, which is why the bus stop was called a “station.” Although connecting services have now been discontinued and ticket sales are no longer handled, the shop that once sold tickets remains in operation, and the bus stop’s name has remained unchanged. The signage on the bus stop pillar has been revised to “Kakeyu Onsen.” See: [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf Meeting Materials 4 – On the Bus Fare Reduction and Operation Plan (Draft)], p.21, 2013, Ueda City; [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf Ueda City Bus Route Map], as of April 1, 2021, Ueda City Transportation Policy Division.
Automobile: About 40 minutes from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft}} |quality = {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft}} |temperature = 42 |flow_rate = |pH = |liquid_type = |osmotic_pressure = |number_of_accommodations = |total_capacity = |annual_number_of_visitors = |statistics_year = |external_link = [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen Homepage|ja|鹿教湯温泉ホームページ}]] |notes = }} '''Kakeyu Onsen''' (鹿教湯温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft}} (formerly {{ill|Maruko Town|ja|丸子町|12=draft}}, formerly {{ill|Nishiuchi Village|ja|西内村|12=draft}}, formerly part of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft}}). == Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Natural spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|12=draft}} {{ill|Temperature|ja|温度|12=draft}} 42℃. ** A colorless, transparent natural spring === Efficacy === It is said to be effective for strokes and high blood pressure. *Note: The stated efficacy does not guarantee results for everyone.* == Hot Spring Town == [[File:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Kakeyu Onsen Ryokan Town]] Along the {{ill|Uchimura River|ja|内村川|12=draft}}, about thirty ryokan and large hotels are located, along with facilities such as {{ill|Rehabilitation|ja|リハビリテーション|lt=Rehabilitation|lt_ja=リハビリ|12=draft}}-centered {{ill|Kakeyu Hospital|ja|鹿教湯病院|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Health Land|ja|健康ランド|lt=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス|12=draft}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|12=draft}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|12=draft}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|deer|ja|シカ|lt=Deer|lt_ja=鹿|12=draft}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=draft}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=draft}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=draft}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国|12=draft}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡|12=draft}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=draft}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅|12=draft}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=draft}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends of Kōbō Daishi]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Legends]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kakeyu Onsen}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|12=draft}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|deer|ja|シカ|lt=Deer|lt_ja=鹿|12=draft}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=draft}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=draft}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=draft}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国|12=draft}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡|12=draft}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=draft}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅|12=draft}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=draft}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Legends]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kakeyu Onsen}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Onsen Legends" to "Onsen Origin Myth"
revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=March 2017|sortkey=温かけゆ}} {{infobox Onsen |name = Kakeyu Onsen (鹿教湯温泉) |image = kakeyu_onsen001.jpg |comment = Hot spring town as seen from the observatory |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft}} |access = Railway: About 45 minutes by bus on the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) from the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft}}; alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stopIn the past, Chikuma Bus operated connecting services with the former {{ill|Japanese National Railways|ja|日本国有鉄道|12=draft}}, and a shop adjacent to this bus stop sold train tickets for the national railway line, which is why the bus stop was called a “station.” Although connecting services have now been discontinued and ticket sales are no longer handled, the shop that once sold tickets remains in operation, and the bus stop’s name has remained unchanged. The signage on the bus stop pillar has been revised to “Kakeyu Onsen.” See: [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf Meeting Materials 4 – On the Bus Fare Reduction and Operation Plan (Draft)], p.21, 2013, Ueda City; [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf Ueda City Bus Route Map], as of April 1, 2021, Ueda City Transportation Policy Division.
Automobile: About 40 minutes from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft}} |quality = {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft}} |temperature = 42 |flow_rate = |pH = |liquid_type = |osmotic_pressure = |number_of_accommodations = |total_capacity = |annual_number_of_visitors = |statistics_year = |external_link = [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen Homepage|ja|鹿教湯温泉ホームページ}]] |notes = }} '''Kakeyu Onsen''' (鹿教湯温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft}} (formerly {{ill|Maruko Town|ja|丸子町|12=draft}}, formerly {{ill|Nishiuchi Village|ja|西内村|12=draft}}, formerly part of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft}}). == Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Natural spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|12=draft}} {{ill|Temperature|ja|温度|12=draft}} 42℃. ** A colorless, transparent natural spring === Efficacy === It is said to be effective for strokes and high blood pressure. *Note: The stated efficacy does not guarantee results for everyone.* == Hot Spring Town == [[File:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Kakeyu Onsen Ryokan Town]] Along the {{ill|Uchimura River|ja|内村川|12=draft}}, about thirty ryokan and large hotels are located, along with facilities such as {{ill|Rehabilitation|ja|リハビリテーション|lt=Rehabilitation|lt_ja=リハビリ|12=draft}}-centered {{ill|Kakeyu Hospital|ja|鹿教湯病院|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Health Land|ja|健康ランド|lt=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス|12=draft}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|12=draft}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|12=draft}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|deer|ja|シカ|lt=Deer|lt_ja=鹿|12=draft}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=draft}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=draft}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=draft}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国|12=draft}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡|12=draft}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=draft}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅|12=draft}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=draft}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kakeyu Onsen}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|12=draft|12=draft}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|deer|ja|シカ|lt=Deer|lt_ja=鹿|12=draft|12=draft}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=draft|12=draft}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft|12=draft}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=draft|12=draft}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=draft|12=draft}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kakeyu Onsen}}

revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=March 2017|sortkey=温かけゆ}} {{infobox Onsen |name = Kakeyu Onsen (鹿教湯温泉) |image = kakeyu_onsen001.jpg |comment = Hot spring town as seen from the observatory |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} |access = Railway: About 45 minutes by bus on the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) from the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}}; alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stopIn the past, Chikuma Bus operated connecting services with the former {{ill|Japanese National Railways|ja|日本国有鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}, and a shop adjacent to this bus stop sold train tickets for the national railway line, which is why the bus stop was called a “station.” Although connecting services have now been discontinued and ticket sales are no longer handled, the shop that once sold tickets remains in operation, and the bus stop’s name has remained unchanged. The signage on the bus stop pillar has been revised to “Kakeyu Onsen.” See: [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf Meeting Materials 4 – On the Bus Fare Reduction and Operation Plan (Draft)], p.21, 2013, Ueda City; [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf Ueda City Bus Route Map], as of April 1, 2021, Ueda City Transportation Policy Division.
Automobile: About 40 minutes from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} |quality = {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} |temperature = 42 |flow_rate = |pH = |liquid_type = |osmotic_pressure = |number_of_accommodations = |total_capacity = |annual_number_of_visitors = |statistics_year = |external_link = [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen Homepage|ja|鹿教湯温泉ホームページ}]] |notes = }} '''Kakeyu Onsen''' (鹿教湯温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly {{ill|Maruko Town|ja|丸子町|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly {{ill|Nishiuchi Village|ja|西内村|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly part of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Natural spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Temperature|ja|温度|12=draft|12=draft}} 42℃. ** A colorless, transparent natural spring === Efficacy === It is said to be effective for strokes and high blood pressure. *Note: The stated efficacy does not guarantee results for everyone.* == Hot Spring Town == [[File:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Kakeyu Onsen Ryokan Town]] Along the {{ill|Uchimura River|ja|内村川|12=draft|12=draft}}, about thirty ryokan and large hotels are located, along with facilities such as {{ill|Rehabilitation|ja|リハビリテーション|lt=Rehabilitation|lt_ja=リハビリ|12=draft|12=draft}}-centered {{ill|Kakeyu Hospital|ja|鹿教湯病院|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Health Land|ja|健康ランド|lt=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス|12=draft|12=draft}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|12=draft|12=draft}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|12=draft|12=draft}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|deer|ja|シカ|lt=Deer|lt_ja=鹿|12=draft|12=draft}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=draft|12=draft}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft|12=draft}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=draft|12=draft}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=draft|12=draft}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kakeyu Onsen}}

{{Unreferenced|date=March 2017|sortkey=温かけゆ}} {{infobox Onsen |name = Kakeyu Onsen (鹿教湯温泉) |image = kakeyu_onsen001.jpg |comment = Hot spring town as seen from the observatory |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} |access = Railway: About 45 minutes by bus on the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) from the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}}; alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stopIn the past, Chikuma Bus operated connecting services with the former {{ill|Japanese National Railways|ja|日本国有鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}, and a shop adjacent to this bus stop sold train tickets for the national railway line, which is why the bus stop was called a “station.” Although connecting services have now been discontinued and ticket sales are no longer handled, the shop that once sold tickets remains in operation, and the bus stop’s name has remained unchanged. The signage on the bus stop pillar has been revised to “Kakeyu Onsen.” See: [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf Meeting Materials 4 – On the Bus Fare Reduction and Operation Plan (Draft)], p.21, 2013, Ueda City; [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf Ueda City Bus Route Map], as of April 1, 2021, Ueda City Transportation Policy Division.
Automobile: About 40 minutes from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} |quality = {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} |temperature = 42 |flow_rate = |pH = |liquid_type = |osmotic_pressure = |number_of_accommodations = |total_capacity = |annual_number_of_visitors = |statistics_year = |external_link = [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen Homepage|ja|鹿教湯温泉ホームページ}]] |notes = }} '''Kakeyu Onsen''' (鹿教湯温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly {{ill|Maruko Town|ja|丸子町|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly {{ill|Nishiuchi Village|ja|西内村|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly part of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Natural spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Temperature|ja|温度|12=draft|12=draft}} 42℃. ** A colorless, transparent natural spring === Efficacy === It is said to be effective for strokes and high blood pressure. *Note: The stated efficacy does not guarantee results for everyone.* == Hot Spring Town == [[File:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Kakeyu Onsen Ryokan Town]] Along the {{ill|Uchimura River|ja|内村川|12=draft|12=draft}}, about thirty ryokan and large hotels are located, along with facilities such as {{ill|Rehabilitation|ja|リハビリテーション|lt=Rehabilitation|lt_ja=リハビリ|12=draft|12=draft}}-centered {{ill|Kakeyu Hospital|ja|鹿教湯病院|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Health Land|ja|健康ランド|lt=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス|12=draft|12=draft}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|12=draft|12=draft}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|12=draft|12=draft}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|deer|ja|シカ|lt=Deer|lt_ja=鹿|12=draft|12=draft}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=draft|12=draft}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft|12=draft}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=draft|12=draft}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=draft|12=draft}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kakeyu Onsen}}

(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kakeyu Onsen}}

{{Unreferenced|date=March 2017|sortkey=温かけゆ}} {{infobox Onsen |name = Kakeyu Onsen (鹿教湯温泉) |image = kakeyu_onsen001.jpg |comment = Hot spring town as seen from the observatory |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} |access = Railway: About 45 minutes by bus on the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) from the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}}; alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stopIn the past, Chikuma Bus operated connecting services with the former {{ill|Japanese National Railways|ja|日本国有鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}, and a shop adjacent to this bus stop sold train tickets for the national railway line, which is why the bus stop was called a “station.” Although connecting services have now been discontinued and ticket sales are no longer handled, the shop that once sold tickets remains in operation, and the bus stop’s name has remained unchanged. The signage on the bus stop pillar has been revised to “Kakeyu Onsen.” See: [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf Meeting Materials 4 – On the Bus Fare Reduction and Operation Plan (Draft)], p.21, 2013, Ueda City; [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf Ueda City Bus Route Map], as of April 1, 2021, Ueda City Transportation Policy Division.
Automobile: About 40 minutes from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} |quality = {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} |temperature = 42 |flow_rate = |pH = |liquid_type = |osmotic_pressure = |number_of_accommodations = |total_capacity = |annual_number_of_visitors = |statistics_year = |external_link = [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen Homepage|ja|鹿教湯温泉ホームページ}]] |notes = }} '''Kakeyu Onsen''' (鹿教湯温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly {{ill|Maruko Town|ja|丸子町|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly {{ill|Nishiuchi Village|ja|西内村|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly part of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Natural spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Temperature|ja|温度|12=draft|12=draft}} 42℃. ** A colorless, transparent natural spring === Efficacy === It is said to be effective for strokes and high blood pressure. *Note: The stated efficacy does not guarantee results for everyone.* == Hot Spring Town == [[File:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Kakeyu Onsen Ryokan Town]] Along the {{ill|Uchimura River|ja|内村川|12=draft|12=draft}}, about thirty ryokan and large hotels are located, along with facilities such as {{ill|Rehabilitation|ja|リハビリテーション|lt=Rehabilitation|lt_ja=リハビリ|12=draft|12=draft}}-centered {{ill|Kakeyu Hospital|ja|鹿教湯病院|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Health Land|ja|健康ランド|lt=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス|12=draft|12=draft}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|12=draft|12=draft}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|12=draft|12=draft}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|deer|ja|シカ|lt=Deer|lt_ja=鹿|12=draft|12=draft}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=draft|12=draft}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft|12=draft}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=draft|12=draft}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=draft|12=draft}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kakeyu Onsen}}

Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=draft|12=draft}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kakeyu Onsen}}

{{Unreferenced|date=March 2017|sortkey=温かけゆ}} {{infobox Onsen |name = Kakeyu Onsen (鹿教湯温泉) |image = kakeyu_onsen001.jpg |comment = Hot spring town as seen from the observatory |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} |access = Railway: About 45 minutes by bus on the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) from the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}}; alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stopIn the past, Chikuma Bus operated connecting services with the former {{ill|Japanese National Railways|ja|日本国有鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}, and a shop adjacent to this bus stop sold train tickets for the national railway line, which is why the bus stop was called a “station.” Although connecting services have now been discontinued and ticket sales are no longer handled, the shop that once sold tickets remains in operation, and the bus stop’s name has remained unchanged. The signage on the bus stop pillar has been revised to “Kakeyu Onsen.” See: [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf Meeting Materials 4 – On the Bus Fare Reduction and Operation Plan (Draft)], p.21, 2013, Ueda City; [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf Ueda City Bus Route Map], as of April 1, 2021, Ueda City Transportation Policy Division.
Automobile: About 40 minutes from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} |quality = {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} |temperature = 42 |flow_rate = |pH = |liquid_type = |osmotic_pressure = |number_of_accommodations = |total_capacity = |annual_number_of_visitors = |statistics_year = |external_link = [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen Homepage|ja|鹿教湯温泉ホームページ}]] |notes = }} '''Kakeyu Onsen''' (鹿教湯温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly {{ill|Maruko Town|ja|丸子町|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly {{ill|Nishiuchi Village|ja|西内村|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly part of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Natural spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Temperature|ja|温度|12=draft|12=draft}} 42℃. ** A colorless, transparent natural spring === Efficacy === It is said to be effective for strokes and high blood pressure. *Note: The stated efficacy does not guarantee results for everyone.* == Hot Spring Town == [[File:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Kakeyu Onsen Ryokan Town]] Along the {{ill|Uchimura River|ja|内村川|12=draft|12=draft}}, about thirty ryokan and large hotels are located, along with facilities such as {{ill|Rehabilitation|ja|リハビリテーション|lt=Rehabilitation|lt_ja=リハビリ|12=draft|12=draft}}-centered {{ill|Kakeyu Hospital|ja|鹿教湯病院|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Health Land|ja|健康ランド|lt=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス|12=draft|12=draft}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|12=draft|12=draft}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|12=draft|12=draft}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|deer|ja|シカ|lt=Deer|lt_ja=鹿|12=draft|12=draft}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=draft|12=draft}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft|12=draft}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=draft|12=draft}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=draft|12=draft}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kakeyu Onsen}}
