Automobile: About 40 minutes from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} |quality = {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} |temperature = 42 |flow_rate = |pH = |liquid_type = |osmotic_pressure = |number_of_accommodations = |total_capacity = |annual_number_of_visitors = |statistics_year = |external_link = [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen Homepage|ja|鹿教湯温泉ホームページ}]] |notes = }} '''Kakeyu Onsen''' (鹿教湯温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly {{ill|Maruko Town|ja|丸子町|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly {{ill|Nishiuchi Village|ja|西内村|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly part of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Natural spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Temperature|ja|温度|12=draft|12=draft}} 42℃. ** A colorless, transparent natural spring === Efficacy === It is said to be effective for strokes and high blood pressure. *Note: The stated efficacy does not guarantee results for everyone.* == Hot Spring Town == [[File:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Kakeyu Onsen Ryokan Town]] Along the {{ill|Uchimura River|ja|内村川|12=draft|12=draft}}, about thirty ryokan and large hotels are located, along with facilities such as {{ill|Rehabilitation|ja|リハビリテーション|lt=Rehabilitation|lt_ja=リハビリ|12=draft|12=draft}}-centered {{ill|Kakeyu Hospital|ja|鹿教湯病院|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Health Land|ja|健康ランド|lt=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス|12=draft|12=draft}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|12=draft|12=draft}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|12=draft|12=draft}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|deer|ja|シカ|lt=Deer|lt_ja=鹿|12=draft|12=draft}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=draft|12=draft}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft|12=draft}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=draft|12=draft}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=draft|12=draft}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kakeyu Onsen}}

{{Unreferenced|date=March 2017|sortkey=温かけゆ}} {{infobox Onsen |name = Kakeyu Onsen (鹿教湯温泉) |image = kakeyu_onsen001.jpg |comment = Hot spring town as seen from the observatory |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} |access = Railway: About 45 minutes by bus on the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) from the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}}; alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stopIn the past, Chikuma Bus operated connecting services with the former {{ill|Japanese National Railways|ja|日本国有鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}, and a shop adjacent to this bus stop sold train tickets for the national railway line, which is why the bus stop was called a “station.” Although connecting services have now been discontinued and ticket sales are no longer handled, the shop that once sold tickets remains in operation, and the bus stop’s name has remained unchanged. The signage on the bus stop pillar has been revised to “Kakeyu Onsen.” See: [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf Meeting Materials 4 – On the Bus Fare Reduction and Operation Plan (Draft)], p.21, 2013, Ueda City; [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf Ueda City Bus Route Map], as of April 1, 2021, Ueda City Transportation Policy Division.

''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kakeyu Onsen}}

{{Unreferenced|date=March 2017|sortkey=温かけゆ}} {{infobox Onsen |name = Kakeyu Onsen (鹿教湯温泉) |image = kakeyu_onsen001.jpg |comment = Hot spring town as seen from the observatory |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} |access = Railway: About 45 minutes by bus on the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) from the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}}; alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stopIn the past, Chikuma Bus operated connecting services with the former {{ill|Japanese National Railways|ja|日本国有鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}, and a shop adjacent to this bus stop sold train tickets for the national railway line, which is why the bus stop was called a “station.” Although connecting services have now been discontinued and ticket sales are no longer handled, the shop that once sold tickets remains in operation, and the bus stop’s name has remained unchanged. The signage on the bus stop pillar has been revised to “Kakeyu Onsen.” See: [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf Meeting Materials 4 – On the Bus Fare Reduction and Operation Plan (Draft)], p.21, 2013, Ueda City; [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf Ueda City Bus Route Map], as of April 1, 2021, Ueda City Transportation Policy Division.
Automobile: About 40 minutes from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} |quality = {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} |temperature = 42 |flow_rate = |pH = |liquid_type = |osmotic_pressure = |number_of_accommodations = |total_capacity = |annual_number_of_visitors = |statistics_year = |external_link = [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen Homepage|ja|鹿教湯温泉ホームページ}]] |notes = }} '''Kakeyu Onsen''' (鹿教湯温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly {{ill|Maruko Town|ja|丸子町|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly {{ill|Nishiuchi Village|ja|西内村|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly part of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Natural spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Temperature|ja|温度|12=draft|12=draft}} 42℃. ** A colorless, transparent natural spring === Efficacy === It is said to be effective for strokes and high blood pressure. *Note: The stated efficacy does not guarantee results for everyone.* == Hot Spring Town == [[File:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Kakeyu Onsen Ryokan Town]] Along the {{ill|Uchimura River|ja|内村川|12=draft|12=draft}}, about thirty ryokan and large hotels are located, along with facilities such as {{ill|Rehabilitation|ja|リハビリテーション|lt=Rehabilitation|lt_ja=リハビリ|12=draft|12=draft}}-centered {{ill|Kakeyu Hospital|ja|鹿教湯病院|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Health Land|ja|健康ランド|lt=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス|12=draft|12=draft}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|12=draft|12=draft}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|12=draft|12=draft}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|deer|ja|シカ|lt=Deer|lt_ja=鹿|12=draft|12=draft}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=draft|12=draft}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft|12=draft}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=draft|12=draft}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=draft|12=draft}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kakeyu Onsen}}

Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=draft|12=draft}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kakeyu Onsen}} {{Kofun navbar}}

{{Unreferenced|date=March 2017|sortkey=温かけゆ}} {{infobox Onsen |name = Kakeyu Onsen (鹿教湯温泉) |image = kakeyu_onsen001.jpg |comment = Hot spring town as seen from the observatory |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} |access = Railway: About 45 minutes by bus on the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) from the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}}; alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stopIn the past, Chikuma Bus operated connecting services with the former {{ill|Japanese National Railways|ja|日本国有鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}, and a shop adjacent to this bus stop sold train tickets for the national railway line, which is why the bus stop was called a “station.” Although connecting services have now been discontinued and ticket sales are no longer handled, the shop that once sold tickets remains in operation, and the bus stop’s name has remained unchanged. The signage on the bus stop pillar has been revised to “Kakeyu Onsen.” See: [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf Meeting Materials 4 – On the Bus Fare Reduction and Operation Plan (Draft)], p.21, 2013, Ueda City; [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf Ueda City Bus Route Map], as of April 1, 2021, Ueda City Transportation Policy Division.
Automobile: About 40 minutes from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} |quality = {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} |temperature = 42 |flow_rate = |pH = |liquid_type = |osmotic_pressure = |number_of_accommodations = |total_capacity = |annual_number_of_visitors = |statistics_year = |external_link = [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen Homepage|ja|鹿教湯温泉ホームページ}]] |notes = }} '''Kakeyu Onsen''' (鹿教湯温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly {{ill|Maruko Town|ja|丸子町|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly {{ill|Nishiuchi Village|ja|西内村|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly part of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Natural spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Temperature|ja|温度|12=draft|12=draft}} 42℃. ** A colorless, transparent natural spring === Efficacy === It is said to be effective for strokes and high blood pressure. *Note: The stated efficacy does not guarantee results for everyone.* == Hot Spring Town == [[File:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Kakeyu Onsen Ryokan Town]] Along the {{ill|Uchimura River|ja|内村川|12=draft|12=draft}}, about thirty ryokan and large hotels are located, along with facilities such as {{ill|Rehabilitation|ja|リハビリテーション|lt=Rehabilitation|lt_ja=リハビリ|12=draft|12=draft}}-centered {{ill|Kakeyu Hospital|ja|鹿教湯病院|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Health Land|ja|健康ランド|lt=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス|12=draft|12=draft}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|12=draft|12=draft}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|12=draft|12=draft}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|deer|ja|シカ|lt=Deer|lt_ja=鹿|12=draft|12=draft}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=draft|12=draft}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft|12=draft}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=draft|12=draft}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=draft|12=draft}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|12=draft|12=draft}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|12=draft|12=draft}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|deer|ja|シカ|lt=Deer|lt_ja=鹿|12=draft|12=draft}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=draft|12=draft}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft|12=draft}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=draft|12=draft}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=draft|12=draft}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{Unreferenced|date=March 2017|sortkey=温かけゆ}} {{infobox Onsen |name = Kakeyu Onsen (鹿教湯温泉) |image = kakeyu_onsen001.jpg |comment = Hot spring town as seen from the observatory |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} |access = Railway: About 45 minutes by bus on the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) from the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}}; alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stopIn the past, Chikuma Bus operated connecting services with the former {{ill|Japanese National Railways|ja|日本国有鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}, and a shop adjacent to this bus stop sold train tickets for the national railway line, which is why the bus stop was called a “station.” Although connecting services have now been discontinued and ticket sales are no longer handled, the shop that once sold tickets remains in operation, and the bus stop’s name has remained unchanged. The signage on the bus stop pillar has been revised to “Kakeyu Onsen.” See: [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf Meeting Materials 4 – On the Bus Fare Reduction and Operation Plan (Draft)], p.21, 2013, Ueda City; [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf Ueda City Bus Route Map], as of April 1, 2021, Ueda City Transportation Policy Division.
Automobile: About 40 minutes from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} |quality = {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} |temperature = 42 |flow_rate = |pH = |liquid_type = |osmotic_pressure = |number_of_accommodations = |total_capacity = |annual_number_of_visitors = |statistics_year = |external_link = [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen Homepage|ja|鹿教湯温泉ホームページ}]] |notes = }} '''Kakeyu Onsen''' (鹿教湯温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly {{ill|Maruko Town|ja|丸子町|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly {{ill|Nishiuchi Village|ja|西内村|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly part of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Natural spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Temperature|ja|温度|12=draft|12=draft}} 42℃. ** A colorless, transparent natural spring === Efficacy === It is said to be effective for strokes and high blood pressure. *Note: The stated efficacy does not guarantee results for everyone.* == Hot Spring Town == [[File:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Kakeyu Onsen Ryokan Town]] Along the {{ill|Uchimura River|ja|内村川|12=draft|12=draft}}, about thirty ryokan and large hotels are located, along with facilities such as {{ill|Rehabilitation|ja|リハビリテーション|lt=Rehabilitation|lt_ja=リハビリ|12=draft|12=draft}}-centered {{ill|Kakeyu Hospital|ja|鹿教湯病院|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Health Land|ja|健康ランド|lt=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス|12=draft|12=draft}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|12=draft|12=draft}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|12=draft|12=draft}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|deer|ja|シカ|lt=Deer|lt_ja=鹿|12=draft|12=draft}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=draft|12=draft}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft|12=draft}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=draft|12=draft}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=draft|12=draft}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{Unreferenced|date=March 2017|sortkey=温かけゆ}} {{infobox Onsen |name = Kakeyu Onsen (鹿教湯温泉) |image = kakeyu_onsen001.jpg |comment = Hot spring town as seen from the observatory |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} |access = Railway: About 45 minutes by bus on the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) from the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}}; alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stopIn the past, Chikuma Bus operated connecting services with the former {{ill|Japanese National Railways|ja|日本国有鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}, and a shop adjacent to this bus stop sold train tickets for the national railway line, which is why the bus stop was called a “station.” Although connecting services have now been discontinued and ticket sales are no longer handled, the shop that once sold tickets remains in operation, and the bus stop’s name has remained unchanged. The signage on the bus stop pillar has been revised to “Kakeyu Onsen.” See: [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf Meeting Materials 4 – On the Bus Fare Reduction and Operation Plan (Draft)], p.21, 2013, Ueda City; [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf Ueda City Bus Route Map], as of April 1, 2021, Ueda City Transportation Policy Division.
Automobile: About 40 minutes from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} |quality = {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} |temperature = 42 |flow_rate = |pH = |liquid_type = |osmotic_pressure = |number_of_accommodations = |total_capacity = |annual_number_of_visitors = |statistics_year = |external_link = [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen Homepage|ja|鹿教湯温泉ホームページ}]] |notes = }} '''Kakeyu Onsen''' (鹿教湯温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly {{ill|Maruko Town|ja|丸子町|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly {{ill|Nishiuchi Village|ja|西内村|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly part of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Natural spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Temperature|ja|温度|12=draft|12=draft}} 42℃. ** A colorless, transparent natural spring === Efficacy === It is said to be effective for strokes and high blood pressure. *Note: The stated efficacy does not guarantee results for everyone.* == Hot Spring Town == [[File:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Kakeyu Onsen Ryokan Town]] Along the {{ill|Uchimura River|ja|内村川|12=draft|12=draft}}, about thirty ryokan and large hotels are located, along with facilities such as {{ill|Rehabilitation|ja|リハビリテーション|lt=Rehabilitation|lt_ja=リハビリ|12=draft|12=draft}}-centered {{ill|Kakeyu Hospital|ja|鹿教湯病院|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Health Land|ja|健康ランド|lt=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス|12=draft|12=draft}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|12=draft|12=draft}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-use_bath_facility|12=draft|12=draft}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|deer|ja|シカ|lt=Deer|lt_ja=鹿|12=draft|12=draft}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=draft|12=draft}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft|12=draft}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=draft|12=draft}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=draft|12=draft}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|健康ランド|" to "|健康ランド|1=Health land|"
revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=March 2017|sortkey=温かけゆ}} {{infobox Onsen |name = Kakeyu Onsen (鹿教湯温泉) |image = kakeyu_onsen001.jpg |comment = Hot spring town as seen from the observatory |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} |access = Railway: About 45 minutes by bus on the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) from the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}}; alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stopIn the past, Chikuma Bus operated connecting services with the former {{ill|Japanese National Railways|ja|日本国有鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}, and a shop adjacent to this bus stop sold train tickets for the national railway line, which is why the bus stop was called a “station.” Although connecting services have now been discontinued and ticket sales are no longer handled, the shop that once sold tickets remains in operation, and the bus stop’s name has remained unchanged. The signage on the bus stop pillar has been revised to “Kakeyu Onsen.” See: [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf Meeting Materials 4 – On the Bus Fare Reduction and Operation Plan (Draft)], p.21, 2013, Ueda City; [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf Ueda City Bus Route Map], as of April 1, 2021, Ueda City Transportation Policy Division.
Automobile: About 40 minutes from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} |quality = {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} |temperature = 42 |flow_rate = |pH = |liquid_type = |osmotic_pressure = |number_of_accommodations = |total_capacity = |annual_number_of_visitors = |statistics_year = |external_link = [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen Homepage|ja|鹿教湯温泉ホームページ}]] |notes = }} '''Kakeyu Onsen''' (鹿教湯温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly {{ill|Maruko Town|ja|丸子町|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly {{ill|Nishiuchi Village|ja|西内村|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly part of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Natural spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Temperature|ja|温度|12=draft|12=draft}} 42℃. ** A colorless, transparent natural spring === Efficacy === It is said to be effective for strokes and high blood pressure. *Note: The stated efficacy does not guarantee results for everyone.* == Hot Spring Town == [[File:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Kakeyu Onsen Ryokan Town]] Along the {{ill|Uchimura River|ja|内村川|12=draft|12=draft}}, about thirty ryokan and large hotels are located, along with facilities such as {{ill|Rehabilitation|ja|リハビリテーション|lt=Rehabilitation|lt_ja=リハビリ|12=draft|12=draft}}-centered {{ill|Kakeyu Hospital|ja|鹿教湯病院|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Health Land|ja|健康ランド|1=Health land|lt=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス|12=draft|12=draft}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|12=draft|12=draft}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facilities|12=draft|12=draft}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|deer|ja|シカ|lt=Deer|lt_ja=鹿|12=draft|12=draft}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=draft|12=draft}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft|12=draft}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=draft|12=draft}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=draft|12=draft}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft|12=draft}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=draft|12=draft}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=draft|12=draft}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|日帰り入浴施設|" to "|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|"
revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=March 2017|sortkey=温かけゆ}} {{infobox Onsen |name = Kakeyu Onsen (鹿教湯温泉) |image = kakeyu_onsen001.jpg |comment = Hot spring town as seen from the observatory |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} |access = Railway: About 45 minutes by bus on the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) from the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}}; alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stopIn the past, Chikuma Bus operated connecting services with the former {{ill|Japanese National Railways|ja|日本国有鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}, and a shop adjacent to this bus stop sold train tickets for the national railway line, which is why the bus stop was called a “station.” Although connecting services have now been discontinued and ticket sales are no longer handled, the shop that once sold tickets remains in operation, and the bus stop’s name has remained unchanged. The signage on the bus stop pillar has been revised to “Kakeyu Onsen.” See: [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf Meeting Materials 4 – On the Bus Fare Reduction and Operation Plan (Draft)], p.21, 2013, Ueda City; [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf Ueda City Bus Route Map], as of April 1, 2021, Ueda City Transportation Policy Division.
Automobile: About 40 minutes from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} |quality = {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} |temperature = 42 |flow_rate = |pH = |liquid_type = |osmotic_pressure = |number_of_accommodations = |total_capacity = |annual_number_of_visitors = |statistics_year = |external_link = [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen Homepage|ja|鹿教湯温泉ホームページ}]] |notes = }} '''Kakeyu Onsen''' (鹿教湯温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly {{ill|Maruko Town|ja|丸子町|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly {{ill|Nishiuchi Village|ja|西内村|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly part of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Natural spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Temperature|ja|温度|12=draft|12=draft}} 42℃. ** A colorless, transparent natural spring === Efficacy === It is said to be effective for strokes and high blood pressure. *Note: The stated efficacy does not guarantee results for everyone.* == Hot Spring Town == [[File:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Kakeyu Onsen Ryokan Town]] Along the {{ill|Uchimura River|ja|内村川|12=draft|12=draft}}, about thirty ryokan and large hotels are located, along with facilities such as {{ill|Rehabilitation|ja|リハビリテーション|lt=Rehabilitation|lt_ja=リハビリ|12=draft|12=draft}}-centered {{ill|Kakeyu Hospital|ja|鹿教湯病院|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Health land|ja|健康ランド|1=Health land|lt=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス|12=draft|12=draft}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|12=draft|12=draft}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|12=draft|12=draft}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|deer|ja|シカ|lt=Deer|lt_ja=鹿|12=draft|12=draft}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=draft|12=draft}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft|12=draft}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=draft|12=draft}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=draft|12=draft}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{Unreferenced|date=March 2017|sortkey=温かけゆ}} {{infobox Onsen |name = Kakeyu Onsen (鹿教湯温泉) |image = kakeyu_onsen001.jpg |comment = Hot spring town as seen from the observatory |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} |access = Railway: About 45 minutes by bus on the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) from the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}}; alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stopIn the past, Chikuma Bus operated connecting services with the former {{ill|Japanese National Railways|ja|日本国有鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}, and a shop adjacent to this bus stop sold train tickets for the national railway line, which is why the bus stop was called a “station.” Although connecting services have now been discontinued and ticket sales are no longer handled, the shop that once sold tickets remains in operation, and the bus stop’s name has remained unchanged. The signage on the bus stop pillar has been revised to “Kakeyu Onsen.” See: [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf Meeting Materials 4 – On the Bus Fare Reduction and Operation Plan (Draft)], p.21, 2013, Ueda City; [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf Ueda City Bus Route Map], as of April 1, 2021, Ueda City Transportation Policy Division.
Automobile: About 40 minutes from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} |quality = {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} |temperature = 42 |flow_rate = |pH = |liquid_type = |osmotic_pressure = |number_of_accommodations = |total_capacity = |annual_number_of_visitors = |statistics_year = |external_link = [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ {{ill|Kakeyu Onsen Homepage|ja|鹿教湯温泉ホームページ}]] |notes = }} '''Kakeyu Onsen''' (鹿教湯温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|12=draft|12=draft}} (formerly {{ill|Maruko Town|ja|丸子町|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly {{ill|Nishiuchi Village|ja|西内村|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly part of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Natural spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Temperature|ja|温度|12=draft|12=draft}} 42℃. ** A colorless, transparent natural spring === Efficacy === It is said to be effective for strokes and high blood pressure. *Note: The stated efficacy does not guarantee results for everyone.* == Hot Spring Town == [[File:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Kakeyu Onsen Ryokan Town]] Along the {{ill|Uchimura River|ja|内村川|12=draft|12=draft}}, about thirty ryokan and large hotels are located, along with facilities such as {{ill|Rehabilitation|ja|リハビリテーション|lt=Rehabilitation|lt_ja=リハビリ|12=draft|12=draft}}-centered {{ill|Kakeyu Hospital|ja|鹿教湯病院|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Health land|ja|健康ランド|1=Health land|lt=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス|12=draft|12=draft}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|12=draft|12=draft}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|12=draft|12=draft}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|deer|ja|シカ|lt=Deer|lt_ja=鹿|12=draft|12=draft}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=draft|12=draft}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft|12=draft}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=draft|12=draft}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=draft|12=draft}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Ueno Province|ja|上野国|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shintan District|ja|新田郡|12=draft|12=draft}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=draft|12=draft}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|ja|北陸新幹線|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|ja|しなの鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station|ja|大屋駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|ja|篠ノ井線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|ja|松本駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] }} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|ja|長野自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. ''' ''' {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun stubs 01-17]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:Onsen Origin Myth]] }} [[sn:Kakeyu Onsen]] [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[sn:chigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017年3月|ソートキー=温かけゆ}} {{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 鹿教湯温泉 |画像 = kakeyu_onsen001.jpg |コメント = 展望台から見る温泉街 |所在地 = [[長野県]][[上田市]] |交通アクセス = 鉄道：[[北陸新幹線]]および[[しなの鉄道]][[上田駅]]より[[千曲バス]]鹿教湯線・上田松本線（土休日のみ）で約45分、「鹿教湯温泉駅（鹿教湯温泉）かつて千曲バスが旧[[日本国有鉄道|国鉄]]と[[連絡運輸]]を行っており、当バス停隣接の商店において国鉄線の乗車券販売を行っていたため、当バス停は「駅」と称している。現在連絡運輸は廃止されており、乗車券の取り扱いも行われていないが、乗車券販売を行っていた商店は営業しており、バス停の名称もそのままとなっている。バス停標識柱の表示は「鹿教湯温泉」に改められている。参考:[https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf 会議資料4　運賃低減バス運行計画（案）について]21頁　2013年　上田市　[https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf 上田市バス路線図]2021年4月1日現在　上田市交通政策課 」バス停下車
自動車：[[長野自動車道]]では[[松本インターチェンジ|松本IC]]または[[安曇野インターチェンジ|安曇野IC]]から40分 |泉質 = [[単純温泉]] |泉温 = 42 |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ 鹿教湯温泉ホームページ]= |特記事項 = }} '''鹿教湯温泉'''（かけゆおんせん）は、[[長野県]][[上田市]]（旧[[丸子町]]、旧[[西内村]]、旧国[[信濃国]]）にある[[温泉]]。 == 泉質 == * 単純温泉 ** [[源泉]][[温度]]42℃。 ** 無色透明の源泉 === 効能 === 中風や高血圧に効能があるとされる。 ※効能はその効果を万人に保証するものではない。 == 温泉街 == [[画像:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|鹿教湯温泉旅館街]] [[内村川]]沿いに約三十軒の旅館や大型ホテルが存在するほか、[[リハビリテーション|リハビリ]]を中心に取り組む[[鹿教湯病院]]や、[[健康ランド|クアハウス]]なども存在する。 [[共同浴場]]は二軒存在する。うち一軒の「文殊の湯」は鹿教湯最古の源泉跡の五台橋のたもとにある。近年改装し、露天風呂も設けられて[[日帰り入浴施設]]に近いような施設となっている。 ほぼ中心に鹿教湯温泉交流センターがあり無料の足湯がある。 付近を内村川などが流れる。 == 歴史 == [[画像:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|落ち着いた川沿いにある鹿教湯温泉[[共同浴場]]]] [[シカ|鹿]]に姿を変えた[[文殊菩薩]]が、信仰心の厚い[[猟師]]に温泉の場所を教えた、という[[開湯伝説]]がある。温泉名もこれに因み、鹿が教えた湯すなわち鹿教湯という。 温泉地にある文殊堂には、[[行基]]が彫った[[文殊菩薩]]像が安置してあり「日本三大文殊」の一つとされる。行基の弟子の[[円行]]がこの地に持ってきた時には既に温泉は湧いており、約1200年前には開湯していたとされる。 温泉の効能から、古くから[[湯治#湯治場|湯治場]]として栄え、江戸時代には[[上野国]][[新田郡]]の[[代官]]が湯治に訪れた記録などが残っている。 [[1956年|昭和31年]][[6月15日]] - [[厚生労働省|厚生省]]告示第152号により、内村温泉の一部として[[霊泉寺温泉]]、[[大塩温泉 (長野県)|大塩温泉]]とともに[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定。指定時は3温泉の総称は内村温泉であったが、のちに丸子温泉郷へと改められている。 == アクセス == * 鉄道：[[北陸新幹線]]および[[しなの鉄道]][[上田駅]]（一部の便を除いて[[大屋駅]]でも接続可能）より[[千曲バス]]鹿教湯線・上田松本線（土休日のみ）で約45分、「鹿教湯温泉駅（鹿教湯温泉）」バス停下車。または、[[篠ノ井線]][[松本駅]]より[[アルピコ交通]]バス（通称・[[松本電鉄バス]]）鹿教湯温泉線（原則平日のみ運行）で約50分、「鹿教湯温泉」バス停下車。
（アルピコ交通で鹿教湯温泉上〜鹿教湯三才山病院口のみの乗車は不可） * 自動車：[[長野自動車道]]では[[松本インターチェンジ|松本IC]]または[[安曇野インターチェンジ|安曇野IC]]から40分。または、[[上信越自動車道]]では[[東部湯の丸インターチェンジ|東部湯の丸IC]]、[[上田菅平インターチェンジ|上田菅平IC]]から40分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ 鹿教湯温泉ホームページ] {{温泉}} {{上田市の町・字}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かけゆおんせん}} [[Category:長野県の温泉]] [[Category:国民保養温泉地]] [[Category:上田市の地理]] [[Category:上田市の町・字]]
