revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Kakeyu Onsen]][[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Kakeyu Onsen]][[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Kakeyu Onsen]][[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kakeyu Onsen]][[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11677198]] Wikidata name: Kakeyu Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11677198]] Wikidata name: Kakeyu Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=March 2017|sortkey=温かけゆ}} {{infobox Onsen |name = Kakeyu Onsen (鹿教湯温泉) |image = kakeyu_onsen001.jpg |comment = Hot spring town as seen from the observatory |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|Ueda, Nagano|WD=Q844852|lt=Ueda City}} |access = Railway: About 45 minutes by bus on the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) from the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|en|Hokuriku Shinkansen|WD=Q1037409|lt=Hokuriku Shinkansen}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway}} {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station}}; alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stopIn the past, Chikuma Bus operated connecting services with the former {{ill|Japanese National Railways|en|Japanese National Railways|WD=Q384011|lt=Japanese National Railways}}, and a shop adjacent to this bus stop sold train tickets for the national railway line, which is why the bus stop was called a “station.” Although connecting services have now been discontinued and ticket sales are no longer handled, the shop that once sold tickets remains in operation, and the bus stop’s name has remained unchanged. The signage on the bus stop pillar has been revised to “Kakeyu Onsen.” See: [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf Meeting Materials 4 – On the Bus Fare Reduction and Operation Plan (Draft)], p.21, 2013, Ueda City; [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf Ueda City Bus Route Map], as of April 1, 2021, Ueda City Transportation Policy Division.
Automobile: About 40 minutes from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|Nagano Expressway|WD=Q6958441|lt=Nagano Expressway}} |quality = {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |temperature = 42 |flow_rate = |pH = |liquid_type = |osmotic_pressure = |number_of_accommodations = |total_capacity = |annual_number_of_visitors = |statistics_year = |external_link = [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Kakeyu Onsen Homepage|ja|鹿教湯温泉ホームページ}]] |notes = }} '''Kakeyu Onsen''' (鹿教湯温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|Ueda, Nagano|WD=Q844852|lt=Ueda City}} (formerly {{ill|Maruko, Nagano|en|Maruko, Nagano|WD=Q6777937|lt=Maruko Town}}, formerly {{ill|Nishiuchi Village|ja|西内村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiuchi Village|1=Nishiuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17987722}}, formerly part of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}}). == Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} ** {{ill|Natural spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} {{ill|Temperature|en|Temperature|WD=Q11466|lt=Temperature}} 42℃. ** A colorless, transparent natural spring === Efficacy === It is said to be effective for strokes and high blood pressure. *Note: The stated efficacy does not guarantee results for everyone.* == Hot Spring Town == [[File:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Kakeyu Onsen Ryokan Town]] Along the {{ill|Uchimura River|ja|内村川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimura River|1=Uchimura River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394053}}, about thirty ryokan and large hotels are located, along with facilities such as {{ill|Rehabilitation|ja|リハビリテーション|lt=Rehabilitation|lt_ja=リハビリ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rehabilitation|1=rehabilitation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2795791}}-centered {{ill|Kakeyu Hospital|ja|鹿教湯病院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakeyu Hospital|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Health land|ja|健康ランド|1=Health land|lt=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Health land|13=User:Immanuelle/Health land|qq=}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/public baths|13=User:Immanuelle/Communal hot springs|qq=}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-use bathing facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|Deer|en|Deer|WD=Q23390|lt=Deer}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hunter|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 狩猟|1=hunting|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q36963}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engyō|1=Engyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394856}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Therapeutics|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湯治|1=thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Ueno Province}} {{ill|Nitta District, Gunma|en|Nitta District, Gunma|WD=Q7041539|lt=Shintan District}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisenzan Onsen|1=Reisenzan Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660743}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|1=Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434139}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|en|Hokuriku Shinkansen|WD=Q1037409|lt=Hokuriku Shinkansen}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway}} {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station (Nagano)|en|Ōya Station (Nagano)|WD=Q8081801|lt=Ōya Station}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|en|Shinonoi Line|WD=Q843201|lt=Shinonoi Line}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|Matsumoto Station|WD=Q843167|lt=Matsumoto Station}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|1=Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531358}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|Nagano Expressway|WD=Q6958441|lt=Nagano Expressway}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|en|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059866|lt=Jōshinetsu Expressway}}. ''' ''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鹿教湯温泉|version=104601729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{Commons category|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11677198}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Geography of Ueda]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Towns and districts of Ueda City]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

Automobile: About 40 minutes from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|Nagano Expressway|WD=Q6958441|lt=Nagano Expressway}} |quality = {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |temperature = 42 |flow_rate = |pH = |liquid_type = |osmotic_pressure = |number_of_accommodations = |total_capacity = |annual_number_of_visitors = |statistics_year = |external_link = [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Kakeyu Onsen Homepage|ja|鹿教湯温泉ホームページ}]] |notes = }} '''Kakeyu Onsen''' (鹿教湯温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|Ueda, Nagano|WD=Q844852|lt=Ueda City}} (formerly {{ill|Maruko, Nagano|en|Maruko, Nagano|WD=Q6777937|lt=Maruko Town}}, formerly {{ill|Nishiuchi Village|ja|西内村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiuchi Village|1=Nishiuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17987722}}, formerly part of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}}). == Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} ** {{ill|Natural spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} {{ill|Temperature|en|Temperature|WD=Q11466|lt=Temperature}} 42℃. ** A colorless, transparent natural spring === Efficacy === It is said to be effective for strokes and high blood pressure. *Note: The stated efficacy does not guarantee results for everyone.* == Hot Spring Town == [[File:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Kakeyu Onsen Ryokan Town]] Along the {{ill|Uchimura River|ja|内村川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimura River|1=Uchimura River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394053}}, about thirty ryokan and large hotels are located, along with facilities such as {{ill|Rehabilitation|ja|リハビリテーション|lt=Rehabilitation|lt_ja=リハビリ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rehabilitation|1=rehabilitation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2795791}}-centered {{ill|Kakeyu Hospital|ja|鹿教湯病院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakeyu Hospital|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Health land|ja|健康ランド|1=Health land|lt=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Health land|13=User:Immanuelle/Health land|qq=}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/public baths|13=User:Immanuelle/Communal hot springs|qq=}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-use bathing facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|Deer|en|Deer|WD=Q23390|lt=Deer}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hunter|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 狩猟|1=hunting|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q36963}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engyō|1=Engyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394856}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Therapeutics|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湯治|1=thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Ueno Province}} {{ill|Nitta District, Gunma|en|Nitta District, Gunma|WD=Q7041539|lt=Shintan District}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisenzan Onsen|1=Reisenzan Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660743}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|1=Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434139}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|en|Hokuriku Shinkansen|WD=Q1037409|lt=Hokuriku Shinkansen}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway}} {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station (Nagano)|en|Ōya Station (Nagano)|WD=Q8081801|lt=Ōya Station}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|en|Shinonoi Line|WD=Q843201|lt=Shinonoi Line}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|Matsumoto Station|WD=Q843167|lt=Matsumoto Station}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|1=Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531358}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|Nagano Expressway|WD=Q6958441|lt=Nagano Expressway}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|en|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059866|lt=Jōshinetsu Expressway}}. ''' ''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鹿教湯温泉|version=104601729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{Commons category|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11677198}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Geography of Ueda]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Towns and districts of Ueda City]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

The signage on the bus stop pillar has been revised to “Kakeyu Onsen.” See: [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf Meeting Materials 4 – On the Bus Fare Reduction and Operation Plan (Draft)], p.21, 2013, Ueda City; [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf Ueda City Bus Route Map], as of April 1, 2021, Ueda City Transportation Policy Division.

At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|en|Hokuriku Shinkansen|WD=Q1037409|lt=Hokuriku Shinkansen}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway}} {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station (Nagano)|en|Ōya Station (Nagano)|WD=Q8081801|lt=Ōya Station}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. land|ltq=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Health land|13=User:Immanuelle/Health land|qq=}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/public baths|13=User:Immanuelle/Communal hot springs|qq=}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-use bathing facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|Deer|en|Deer|WD=Q23390|lt=Deer}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hunter|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 狩猟|1=hunting|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q36963}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engyō|1=Engyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394856}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Therapeutics|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湯治|1=thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Ueno Province}} {{ill|Nitta District, Gunma|en|Nitta District, Gunma|WD=Q7041539|lt=Shintan District}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisenzan Onsen|1=Reisenzan Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660743}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|1=Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434139}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|en|Hokuriku Shinkansen|WD=Q1037409|lt=Hokuriku Shinkansen}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway}} {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station (Nagano)|en|Ōya Station (Nagano)|WD=Q8081801|lt=Ōya Station}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|en|Shinonoi Line|WD=Q843201|lt=Shinonoi Line}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|Matsumoto Station|WD=Q843167|lt=Matsumoto Station}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|1=Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531358}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|Nagano Expressway|WD=Q6958441|lt=Nagano Expressway}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|en|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059866|lt=Jōshinetsu Expressway}}. ''' ''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鹿教湯温泉|version=104601729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{Commons category|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11677198}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Geography of Ueda]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Towns and districts of Ueda City]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kakeyu Onsen]][[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Kakeyu Onsen]][[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Kakeyu Onsen]][[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kakeyu Onsen]][[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] {{Unreferenced|date=March 2017|sortkey=温かけゆ}} {{infobox Onsen |name = Kakeyu Onsen (鹿教湯温泉) |image = kakeyu_onsen001.jpg |comment = Hot spring town as seen from the observatory |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|Ueda, Nagano|WD=Q844852|lt=Ueda City}} |access = Railway: About 45 minutes by bus on the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) from the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|en|Hokuriku Shinkansen|WD=Q1037409|lt=Hokuriku Shinkansen}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway}} {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station}}; alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stopIn the past, Chikuma Bus operated connecting services with the former {{ill|Japanese National Railways|en|Japanese National Railways|WD=Q384011|lt=Japanese National Railways}}, and a shop adjacent to this bus stop sold train tickets for the national railway line, which is why the bus stop was called a “station.” Although connecting services have now been discontinued and ticket sales are no longer handled, the shop that once sold tickets remains in operation, and the bus stop’s name has remained unchanged. The signage on the bus stop pillar has been revised to “Kakeyu Onsen.” See: [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf Meeting Materials 4 – On the Bus Fare Reduction and Operation Plan (Draft)], p.21, 2013, Ueda City; [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf Ueda City Bus Route Map], as of April 1, 2021, Ueda City Transportation Policy Division.
Automobile: About 40 minutes from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|Nagano Expressway|WD=Q6958441|lt=Nagano Expressway}} |quality = {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |temperature = 42 |flow_rate = |pH = |liquid_type = |osmotic_pressure = |number_of_accommodations = |total_capacity = |annual_number_of_visitors = |statistics_year = |external_link = [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Kakeyu Onsen Homepage|ja|鹿教湯温泉ホームページ}]] |notes = }} '''Kakeyu Onsen''' (鹿教湯温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|Ueda, Nagano|WD=Q844852|lt=Ueda City}} (formerly {{ill|Maruko, Nagano|en|Maruko, Nagano|WD=Q6777937|lt=Maruko Town}}, formerly {{ill|Nishiuchi Village|ja|西内村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiuchi Village|1=Nishiuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17987722}}, formerly part of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}}). == Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} ** {{ill|Natural spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} {{ill|Temperature|en|Temperature|WD=Q11466|lt=Temperature}} 42℃. ** A colorless, transparent natural spring === Efficacy === It is said to be effective for strokes and high blood pressure. *Note: The stated efficacy does not guarantee results for everyone.* == Hot Spring Town == [[File:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Kakeyu Onsen Ryokan Town]] Along the {{ill|Uchimura River|ja|内村川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimura River|1=Uchimura River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394053}}, about thirty ryokan and large hotels are located, along with facilities such as {{ill|Rehabilitation|ja|リハビリテーション|lt=Rehabilitation|lt_ja=リハビリ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rehabilitation|1=rehabilitation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2795791}}-centered {{ill|Kakeyu Hospital|ja|鹿教湯病院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakeyu Hospital|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Health land|ja|健康ランド|1=Health land|lt=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Health land|13=User:Immanuelle/Health land|qq=}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/public baths|13=User:Immanuelle/Communal hot springs|qq=}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-use bathing facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|Deer|en|Deer|WD=Q23390|lt=Deer}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hunter|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 狩猟|1=hunting|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q36963}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engyō|1=Engyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394856}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Therapeutics|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湯治|1=thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Ueno Province}} {{ill|Nitta District, Gunma|en|Nitta District, Gunma|WD=Q7041539|lt=Shintan District}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisenzan Onsen|1=Reisenzan Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660743}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|1=Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434139}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|en|Hokuriku Shinkansen|WD=Q1037409|lt=Hokuriku Shinkansen}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway}} {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station (Nagano)|en|Ōya Station (Nagano)|WD=Q8081801|lt=Ōya Station}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|en|Shinonoi Line|WD=Q843201|lt=Shinonoi Line}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|Matsumoto Station|WD=Q843167|lt=Matsumoto Station}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|1=Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531358}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|Nagano Expressway|WD=Q6958441|lt=Nagano Expressway}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|en|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059866|lt=Jōshinetsu Expressway}}. ''' ''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鹿教湯温泉|version=104601729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{Commons category|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11677198}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Geography of Ueda]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Towns and districts of Ueda City]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:pages]]

In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engyō|1=Engyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394856}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Therapeutics|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湯治|1=Thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Ueno Province|1=Kōzuke Province}} {{ill|Nitta District, Gunma|en|Nitta District, Gunma|WD=Q7041539|lt=Shintan District|1=Nitta District, Gunma}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisenzan Onsen|1=Reisenzan Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660743}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|1=Q11434139|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434139}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|en|Hokuriku Shinkansen|WD=Q1037409|lt=Hokuriku Shinkansen|1=Hokuriku Shinkansen}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway|1=Shinano Railway}} {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station|1=Ueda Station (Nagano)}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station (Nagano)|en|Ōya Station (Nagano)|WD=Q8081801|lt=Ōya Station|1=Ōya Station (Nagano)}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|en|Shinonoi Line|WD=Q843201|lt=Shinonoi Line|1=Shinonoi Line}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|Matsumoto Station|WD=Q843167|lt=Matsumoto Station|1=Matsumoto Station}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|1=Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531358}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|Nagano Expressway|WD=Q6958441|lt=Nagano Expressway|1=Nagano Expressway}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|en|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059866|lt=Jōshinetsu Expressway|1=Jōshin-etsu Expressway}}. ''' ''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鹿教湯温泉|version=104601729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{Commons category|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11677198}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Geography of Ueda]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Towns and districts of Ueda City]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kakeyu Onsen]][[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Kakeyu Onsen]][[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Kakeyu Onsen]][[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[Category:sn_crud commons:Category:Kakeyu Onsen]][[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] {{Unreferenced|date=March 2017|sortkey=温かけゆ}} {{infobox Onsen |name = Kakeyu Onsen (鹿教湯温泉) |image = kakeyu_onsen001.jpg |comment = Hot spring town as seen from the observatory |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|Ueda, Nagano|WD=Q844852|lt=Ueda City|1=Ueda, Nagano}} |access = Railway: About 45 minutes by bus on the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) from the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|en|Hokuriku Shinkansen|WD=Q1037409|lt=Hokuriku Shinkansen|1=Hokuriku Shinkansen}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway|1=Shinano Railway}} {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station|1=Ueda Station (Nagano)}}; alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stopIn the past, Chikuma Bus operated connecting services with the former {{ill|Japanese National Railways|en|Japanese National Railways|WD=Q384011|lt=Japanese National Railways|1=Japanese National Railways}}, and a shop adjacent to this bus stop sold train tickets for the national railway line, which is why the bus stop was called a “station.” Although connecting services have now been discontinued and ticket sales are no longer handled, the shop that once sold tickets remains in operation, and the bus stop’s name has remained unchanged. The signage on the bus stop pillar has been revised to “Kakeyu Onsen.” See: [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf Meeting Materials 4 – On the Bus Fare Reduction and Operation Plan (Draft)], p.21, 2013, Ueda City; [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf Ueda City Bus Route Map], as of April 1, 2021, Ueda City Transportation Policy Division.

spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |temperature = 42 |flow_rate = |pH = |liquid_type = |osmotic_pressure = |number_of_accommodations = |total_capacity = |annual_number_of_visitors = |statistics_year = |external_link = [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Kakeyu Onsen Homepage|ja|鹿教湯温泉ホームページ}]] |notes = }} '''Kakeyu Onsen''' (鹿教湯温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|Ueda, Nagano|WD=Q844852|lt=Ueda City|1=Ueda, Nagano}} (formerly {{ill|Maruko, Nagano|en|Maruko, Nagano|WD=Q6777937|lt=Maruko Town|1=Maruko, Nagano}}, formerly {{ill|Nishiuchi Village|ja|西内村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiuchi Village|1=Nishiuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17987722}}, formerly part of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}). == Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} ** {{ill|Natural spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} {{ill|Temperature|en|Temperature|WD=Q11466|lt=Temperature|1=Temperature}} 42℃. ** A colorless, transparent natural spring === Efficacy === It is said to be effective for strokes and high blood pressure. *Note: The stated efficacy does not guarantee results for everyone.* == Hot Spring Town == [[File:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Kakeyu Onsen Ryokan Town]] Along the {{ill|Uchimura River|ja|内村川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimura River|1=Uchimura River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394053}}, about thirty ryokan and large hotels are located, along with facilities such as {{ill|Rehabilitation|ja|リハビリテーション|lt=Rehabilitation|lt_ja=リハビリ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rehabilitation|1=rehabilitation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2795791}}-centered {{ill|Kakeyu Hospital|ja|鹿教湯病院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakeyu Hospital|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Health land|ja|健康ランド|1=Health land|lt=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Health land|13=User:Immanuelle/Health land|qq=}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/public baths|13=User:Immanuelle/Communal hot springs|qq=}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-use bathing facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|Deer|en|Deer|WD=Q23390|lt=Deer|1=Deer}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hunter|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 狩猟|1=Hunting|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q36963}}. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Therapeutics|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湯治|1=Thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Ueno Province|1=Kōzuke Province}} {{ill|Nitta District, Gunma|en|Nitta District, Gunma|WD=Q7041539|lt=Shintan District|1=Nitta District, Gunma}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisenzan Onsen|1=Reisenzan Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660743}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|1=Q11434139|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434139}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|en|Hokuriku Shinkansen|WD=Q1037409|lt=Hokuriku Shinkansen|1=Hokuriku Shinkansen}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway|1=Shinano Railway}} {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station|1=Ueda Station (Nagano)}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station (Nagano)|en|Ōya Station (Nagano)|WD=Q8081801|lt=Ōya Station|1=Ōya Station (Nagano)}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|en|Shinonoi Line|WD=Q843201|lt=Shinonoi Line|1=Shinonoi Line}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|Matsumoto Station|WD=Q843167|lt=Matsumoto Station|1=Matsumoto Station}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|1=Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531358}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.

On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisenzan Onsen|1=Reisenzan Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660743}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|1=Q11434139|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434139}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|en|Hokuriku Shinkansen|WD=Q1037409|lt=Hokuriku Shinkansen|1=Hokuriku Shinkansen}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway|1=Shinano Railway}} {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station|1=Ueda Station (Nagano)}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station (Nagano)|en|Ōya Station (Nagano)|WD=Q8081801|lt=Ōya Station|1=Ōya Station (Nagano)}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|en|Shinonoi Line|WD=Q843201|lt=Shinonoi Line|1=Shinonoi Line}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|Matsumoto Station|WD=Q843167|lt=Matsumoto Station|1=Matsumoto Station}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|1=Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531358}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|Nagano Expressway|WD=Q6958441|lt=Nagano Expressway|1=Nagano Expressway}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|en|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059866|lt=Jōshinetsu Expressway|1=Jōshin-etsu Expressway}}. ''' ''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鹿教湯温泉|version=104601729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{Commons category|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11677198}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Geography of Ueda]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Towns and districts of Ueda City]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kakeyu Onsen]][[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Kakeyu Onsen]][[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Kakeyu Onsen]][[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kakeyu Onsen]][[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] {{Unreferenced|date=March 2017|sortkey=温かけゆ}} {{infobox Onsen |name = Kakeyu Onsen (鹿教湯温泉) |image = kakeyu_onsen001.jpg |comment = Hot spring town as seen from the observatory |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|Ueda, Nagano|WD=Q844852|lt=Ueda City|1=Ueda, Nagano}} |access = Railway: About 45 minutes by bus on the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) from the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|en|Hokuriku Shinkansen|WD=Q1037409|lt=Hokuriku Shinkansen|1=Hokuriku Shinkansen}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway|1=Shinano Railway}} {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station|1=Ueda Station (Nagano)}}; alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stopIn the past, Chikuma Bus operated connecting services with the former {{ill|Japanese National Railways|en|Japanese National Railways|WD=Q384011|lt=Japanese National Railways|1=Japanese National Railways}}, and a shop adjacent to this bus stop sold train tickets for the national railway line, which is why the bus stop was called a “station.” Although connecting services have now been discontinued and ticket sales are no longer handled, the shop that once sold tickets remains in operation, and the bus stop’s name has remained unchanged. The signage on the bus stop pillar has been revised to “Kakeyu Onsen.” See: [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf Meeting Materials 4 – On the Bus Fare Reduction and Operation Plan (Draft)], p.21, 2013, Ueda City; [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf Ueda City Bus Route Map], as of April 1, 2021, Ueda City Transportation Policy Division.
Automobile: About 40 minutes from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|Nagano Expressway|WD=Q6958441|lt=Nagano Expressway|1=Nagano Expressway}} |quality = {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |temperature = 42 |flow_rate = |pH = |liquid_type = |osmotic_pressure = |number_of_accommodations = |total_capacity = |annual_number_of_visitors = |statistics_year = |external_link = [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Kakeyu Onsen Homepage|ja|鹿教湯温泉ホームページ}]] |notes = }} '''Kakeyu Onsen''' (鹿教湯温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|Ueda, Nagano|WD=Q844852|lt=Ueda City|1=Ueda, Nagano}} (formerly {{ill|Maruko, Nagano|en|Maruko, Nagano|WD=Q6777937|lt=Maruko Town|1=Maruko, Nagano}}, formerly {{ill|Nishiuchi Village|ja|西内村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiuchi Village|1=Nishiuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17987722}}, formerly part of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}). == Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} ** {{ill|Natural spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} {{ill|Temperature|en|Temperature|WD=Q11466|lt=Temperature|1=Temperature}} 42℃. ** A colorless, transparent natural spring === Efficacy === It is said to be effective for strokes and high blood pressure. *Note: The stated efficacy does not guarantee results for everyone.* == Hot Spring Town == [[File:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Kakeyu Onsen Ryokan Town]] Along the {{ill|Uchimura River|ja|内村川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimura River|1=Uchimura River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394053}}, about thirty ryokan and large hotels are located, along with facilities such as {{ill|Rehabilitation|ja|リハビリテーション|lt=Rehabilitation|lt_ja=リハビリ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rehabilitation|1=rehabilitation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2795791}}-centered {{ill|Kakeyu Hospital|ja|鹿教湯病院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakeyu Hospital|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Health land|ja|健康ランド|1=Health land|lt=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Health land|13=User:Immanuelle/Health land|qq=}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/public baths|13=User:Immanuelle/Communal hot springs|qq=}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-use bathing facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|Deer|en|Deer|WD=Q23390|lt=Deer|1=Deer}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hunter|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 狩猟|1=Hunting|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q36963}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engyō|1=Engyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394856}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Therapeutics|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湯治|1=Thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Ueno Province|1=Kōzuke Province}} {{ill|Nitta District, Gunma|en|Nitta District, Gunma|WD=Q7041539|lt=Shintan District|1=Nitta District, Gunma}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisenzan Onsen|1=Reisenzan Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660743}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|1=Q11434139|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434139}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|en|Hokuriku Shinkansen|WD=Q1037409|lt=Hokuriku Shinkansen|1=Hokuriku Shinkansen}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway|1=Shinano Railway}} {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station|1=Ueda Station (Nagano)}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station (Nagano)|en|Ōya Station (Nagano)|WD=Q8081801|lt=Ōya Station|1=Ōya Station (Nagano)}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|en|Shinonoi Line|WD=Q843201|lt=Shinonoi Line|1=Shinonoi Line}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|Matsumoto Station|WD=Q843167|lt=Matsumoto Station|1=Matsumoto Station}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|1=Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531358}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|Nagano Expressway|WD=Q6958441|lt=Nagano Expressway|1=Nagano Expressway}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|en|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059866|lt=Jōshinetsu Expressway|1=Jōshin-etsu Expressway}}. ''' ''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鹿教湯温泉|version=104601729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{Commons category|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11677198}} [[Category:Geography of Ueda]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Towns and districts of Ueda City]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:pages]]

At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|en|Hokuriku Shinkansen|WD=Q1037409|lt=Hokuriku Shinkansen|1=Hokuriku Shinkansen}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway|1=Shinano Railway}} {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station|1=Ueda Station (Nagano)}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station (Nagano)|en|Ōya Station (Nagano)|WD=Q8081801|lt=Ōya Station|1=Ōya Station (Nagano)}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|en|Shinonoi Line|WD=Q843201|lt=Shinonoi Line|1=Shinonoi Line}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|Matsumoto Station|WD=Q843167|lt=Matsumoto Station|1=Matsumoto Station}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|1=Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531358}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|Nagano Expressway|WD=Q6958441|lt=Nagano Expressway|1=Nagano Expressway}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|en|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059866|lt=Jōshinetsu Expressway|1=Jōshin-etsu Expressway}}. ''' ''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鹿教湯温泉|version=104601729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{Commons category|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11677198}} [[Category:Geography of Ueda]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Towns and districts of Ueda City]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Kakeyu Onsen]][[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Kakeyu Onsen]][[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[co:commons:Category:Kakeyu Onsen]][[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kakeyu Onsen]][[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] {{Unreferenced|date=March 2017|sortkey=温かけゆ}} {{infobox Onsen |name = Kakeyu Onsen (鹿教湯温泉) |image = kakeyu_onsen001.jpg |comment = Hot spring town as seen from the observatory |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|Ueda, Nagano|WD=Q844852|lt=Ueda City|1=Ueda, Nagano}} |access = Railway: About 45 minutes by bus on the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) from the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|en|Hokuriku Shinkansen|WD=Q1037409|lt=Hokuriku Shinkansen|1=Hokuriku Shinkansen}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway|1=Shinano Railway}} {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station|1=Ueda Station (Nagano)}}; alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stopIn the past, Chikuma Bus operated connecting services with the former {{ill|Japanese National Railways|en|Japanese National Railways|WD=Q384011|lt=Japanese National Railways|1=Japanese National Railways}}, and a shop adjacent to this bus stop sold train tickets for the national railway line, which is why the bus stop was called a “station.” Although connecting services have now been discontinued and ticket sales are no longer handled, the shop that once sold tickets remains in operation, and the bus stop’s name has remained unchanged. The signage on the bus stop pillar has been revised to “Kakeyu Onsen.” See: [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/4826.pdf Meeting Materials 4 – On the Bus Fare Reduction and Operation Plan (Draft)], p.21, 2013, Ueda City; [https://www.city.ueda.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/30835.pdf Ueda City Bus Route Map], as of April 1, 2021, Ueda City Transportation Policy Division.
Automobile: About 40 minutes from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|Nagano Expressway|WD=Q6958441|lt=Nagano Expressway|1=Nagano Expressway}} |quality = {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |temperature = 42 |flow_rate = |pH = |liquid_type = |osmotic_pressure = |number_of_accommodations = |total_capacity = |annual_number_of_visitors = |statistics_year = |external_link = [http://www.kakeyu.or.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Kakeyu Onsen Homepage|ja|鹿教湯温泉ホームページ}]] |notes = }} '''Kakeyu Onsen''' (鹿教湯温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|Ueda, Nagano|WD=Q844852|lt=Ueda City|1=Ueda, Nagano}} (formerly {{ill|Maruko, Nagano|en|Maruko, Nagano|WD=Q6777937|lt=Maruko Town|1=Maruko, Nagano}}, formerly {{ill|Nishiuchi Village|ja|西内村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiuchi Village|1=Nishiuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17987722}}, formerly part of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}). == Hot Spring Quality == * {{ill|Simple hot spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} ** {{ill|Natural spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} {{ill|Temperature|en|Temperature|WD=Q11466|lt=Temperature|1=Temperature}} 42℃. ** A colorless, transparent natural spring === Efficacy === It is said to be effective for strokes and high blood pressure. *Note: The stated efficacy does not guarantee results for everyone.* == Hot Spring Town == [[File:Kakeyuonsen-street鹿教湯温泉街.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Kakeyu Onsen Ryokan Town]] Along the {{ill|Uchimura River|ja|内村川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimura River|1=Uchimura River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394053}}, about thirty ryokan and large hotels are located, along with facilities such as {{ill|Rehabilitation|ja|リハビリテーション|lt=Rehabilitation|lt_ja=リハビリ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rehabilitation|1=rehabilitation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2795791}}-centered {{ill|Kakeyu Hospital|ja|鹿教湯病院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakeyu Hospital|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Health land|ja|健康ランド|1=Health land|lt=Quahouse|lt_ja=クアハウス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Health land|13=User:Immanuelle/Health land|qq=}}. There are two {{ill|public baths|ja|共同浴場|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|1=Communal hot springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/public baths|13=User:Immanuelle/Communal hot springs|qq=}}. One of these, “Monju no Yu,” is situated at the foot of Godai Bridge – the site of the oldest natural spring in Kakeyu. Recently renovated, it now features an open-air bath and has become similar to a day-use bathing facility ({{ill|Day-use bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-use bathing facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}). == History == [[File:Kakeyu-onsen-kyoudoyokujo鹿教湯温泉共同浴場.jpg|thumbnail|200px|A tranquil public bath along the riverside at Kakeyu Onsen]] According to a legend of the hot spring’s founding, Monju Bosatsu, having transformed into a {{ill|Deer|en|Deer|WD=Q23390|lt=Deer|1=Deer}}, once revealed the location of the hot spring to a devout {{ill|hunter|ja|猟師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hunter|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 狩猟|1=Hunting|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q36963}}. In keeping with this legend, the hot spring is named “Kakeyu” – meaning “the hot water taught by the deer.” At the Monju Hall in the hot spring area, a statue of Monju Bosatsu carved by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}} is enshrined, and it is regarded as one of “Japan’s Three Great Monju.” When Gyōki’s disciple, {{ill|Engyō|ja|円行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engyō|1=Engyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394856}}, brought the statue to this area, the hot spring was already in flow; it is said that the spring was established about 1200 years ago. Thanks to its reputed healing properties, the area has long flourished as a spa resort ({{ill|Onsen Therapeutics|ja|湯治場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Therapeutics|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湯治|1=Thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1004887}}), with records from the Edo period noting that daikan from {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Ueno Province|1=Kōzuke Province}} {{ill|Nitta District, Gunma|en|Nitta District, Gunma|WD=Q7041539|lt=Shintan District|1=Nitta District, Gunma}} visited for hot spring cures. On June 15, 1956 (Showa 31), by Notice No. 152 of the Ministry of Health and Welfare (then the Ministry of Health), Kakeyu Onsen – along with {{ill|Reisenzan Onsen|ja|霊泉寺温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisenzan Onsen|1=Reisenzan Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660743}} and {{ill|Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|ja|大塩温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshio Onsen (Nagano)|1=Q11434139|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434139}} – was designated as a National Health Resort area as part of Uchimura Onsen. At the time of designation, the three hot springs were collectively known as Uchimura Onsen, but later the name was changed to Maruko Onsen Town. == Access == * Railway: From the {{ill|Hokuriku Shinkansen|en|Hokuriku Shinkansen|WD=Q1037409|lt=Hokuriku Shinkansen|1=Hokuriku Shinkansen}} and {{ill|Shinano Railway|en|Shinano Railway|WD=Q11254401|lt=Shinano Railway|1=Shinano Railway}} {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station|1=Ueda Station (Nagano)}} (connections are also available from {{ill|Ōya Station (Nagano)|en|Ōya Station (Nagano)|WD=Q8081801|lt=Ōya Station|1=Ōya Station (Nagano)}} on some services), take the Chikuma Bus Kakeyu Line/Ueda–Matsumoto Line (operating only on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays) for about 45 minutes and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen Station (Kakeyu Onsen)” bus stop. Alternatively, from the {{ill|Shinonoi Line|en|Shinonoi Line|WD=Q843201|lt=Shinonoi Line|1=Shinonoi Line}} {{ill|Matsumoto Station|en|Matsumoto Station|WD=Q843167|lt=Matsumoto Station|1=Matsumoto Station}}, take an Alpico Transportation bus (commonly known as {{ill|Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|ja|松本電鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|1=Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11531358}}) on the Kakeyu Onsen Line (generally operating only on weekdays) for about 50 minutes, and alight at the “Kakeyu Onsen” bus stop.
(Note: It is not possible to board only the segment between Kakeyu Onsen Ue and Kakeyu Onsen Sansaiyama Hospital Entrance on the Alpico Transportation service.) * Automobile: About 40 minutes by car from Matsumoto IC or Azumino IC on the {{ill|Nagano Expressway|en|Nagano Expressway|WD=Q6958441|lt=Nagano Expressway|1=Nagano Expressway}}, or about 40 minutes from Higashi-Yunomaru IC or Ueda Sugata IC on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|en|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059866|lt=Jōshinetsu Expressway|1=Jōshin-etsu Expressway}}. ''' ''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鹿教湯温泉|version=104601729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja: 鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:%E5%B8%82%E3%83%B6%E5%B0%BE%E6%A8%AA%E7%A9%B4%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4]] [[zh:温泉]] [[ja:鹿教湯温泉]] [[ja:俵山温泉]] [[ja:老神温泉]] [[zh:温泉神社]] [[zh:温泉寺]] [[ja:開湯伝説]] {{Commons category|Kakeyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11677198}} [[Category:Geography of Ueda]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Legends]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Towns and districts of Ueda City]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]
